acceptance.note: PN248 acceptance distribution not recorded for this artifact-creation bench. Operator may collect via SNDR_ENABLE_SPEC_DECODE_ACCEPTANCE_METRIC=1 and refresh; per-class TPS measurements below are the load-bearing evidence.

notes: K=3 chat-role bench 2026-05-31 on pin 0.21.1rc1.dev354+g626fa9bba. Mirror-sibling of gemma4-26b-mtp-k4: K=4 was measured against the same prompt suite and shows the inverse trade-off (free_chat -13.0%, summarization -9.8%, code_gen +4.9%, structured_count +7.5%, tool_json +0.5%; geomean -2.3%). K=3 chat profile is +13.1% on free-form mean and +2.4% on global geomean vs the K=4 sibling — modest but consistent in the same direction as 31B (where K=3 vs K=4 was +105.7% / +19.0% — much larger because the 31B drafter has lower per-token acceptance on chat). Allowed_workloads narrowed to free_chat + summarization (where K=3 wins ≥ +7% vs K=4); code_gen excluded because K=4 wins it (+4.9%); structured workloads delegated to the K=4 sibling.
